South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in January and February, missing forecasts of 2.4% but exceeding the Bank of Korea's (BOK) 2.0% target due to rising oil prices from Middle East conflicts and a weakening won.[2][4][5] This follows BOK'
